overview: Azorius Ephemerate is a control/tempo deck that leverages cheap disruption and card selection to dictate the pace of the game. By holding up mana for Counterspell and Preordain, you can safely navigate the early turns while setting up powerful board clears or permanent removal. The deck aims to stabilize by turn four, then use Ephemerate to repeatedly exile key threats and generate massive card advantage.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through superior card advantage
Exiling key threats with Ephemerate to clear the board
Winning with a resilient late-game plan or combat tricks

keep_criteria: Hands with Preordain and a cheap counter or removal
Two-mana spells that can interact on turn one or two
Mana bases that support early W/U spells
Keep hands with 2 lands (including at least one of each color or duals), Preordain, and Counterspell.
Keep any hand with Preordain + land, or Counterspell + land.
Avoid keeping pure curve-out hands without answers, or hands with 3+ lands and no spells.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking interaction or card draw
Mana screws with too many lands or too few
Hands that cannot play anything before turn three
Mulligan to 4+ lands if you have no early interaction.

early_game: Play lands and Preordain to filter your deck
Hold up mana for Counterspell to protect your resources
Turn 1: Play land, cast Preordain to set up land/answer/threat.
Turn 2: Play second land, cast Counterspell if opponent casts a key spell, or play Ephemerate to bounce a threat.
Prioritize maintaining card advantage and answering key threats before committing to a board.
If no interaction is needed, develop a cheap creature or spell while holding up mana for counters.
mid_game: Use Ephemerate on high-value threats or board sweepers
Cycle through your deck to find answers while maintaining mana advantage
Start building a board presence if the opponent stalls
Hoard counterspells for key threats, use Preordain to dig for answers or threats, and avoid overcommitting mana.
Keep 1-2 counterspells in hand at all times.
Manage your graveyard by avoiding unnecessary scry/dig if the opponent has hate, and use Ephemerate to protect your hand or board when resources are tight.
Hold back on committing multiple threats until you have interaction or a clear path to closing.
Use Ephemerate defensively to bounce removal targets or offensively to trigger ETBs.
Only commit when you can protect your board or have enough interaction to respond to their answers.
late_game: Replay Ephemerate on recurring threats or finishers
Leverage card advantage to out-resource the opponent
Close out the game with efficient removal or combat damage
Ephemerate bouncing a key creature to trigger ETB effects, or bouncing an opponent's threat to stabilize and close with card advantage.
Secondary finishers include efficient removal and counterspells to protect a closing threat or force a favorable trade.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, the opponent has no counterspells, or you can safely resolve a game-ending threat.
Also commit when you can bounce a threat and still have mana for interaction, or when you can force them to use multiple answers on a single threat.

key_cards.name: Ephemerate
key_cards.role: Primary disruption and card advantage engine
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn four to exile a threat for four turns, then replay it to exile another or the same card for massive advantage.

key_cards.name: Counterspell
key_cards.role: Early game interaction and tempo tool
key_cards.notes: Hold up one blue mana to counter key spells, protecting your board state and buying time to draw into answers.

key_cards.name: Preordain
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn one to find lands, counterspells, or Ephemerate, ensuring you never get stuck without answers.

sequencing_tips: Always prioritize Preordain on turn one to optimize your hand
Hold up mana for Counterspell before playing lands or other spells
Delay Ephemerate until you can replay it for maximum value
Save counters for game-ending threats rather than early fliers
Ephemerate is versatile but not a win-con on its own
pair it with creatures that benefit from being cast multiple times or use it defensively to stabilize.
save it for when you need to dig for answers or threats.
Watch for opponent's mana development to predict their interaction.
Common mistake: holding too many counterspells and running out of mana to cast threats, or using Ephemerate on non-essential targets when you could save it for a game-ending bounce.

matchups.advice: Prioritize Counterspell on early threats and use Ephemerate on their win conditions.
watch_for: Bypassing counters with haste or enter-the-battlefield effects

matchups.advice: Race them by finding answers to their sweepers and using Ephemerate to stall their finishers.
Use Counterspell on key combo pieces and Preordain to find specific answers.
watch_for: Winning on turn three or four

matchups.advice: Use Preordain to dig around counterspells, cast threats in response to their counters, or layer interaction to force them to use multiple spells. Save Ephemerate to bounce their countered spells if possible, or use it to protect your own threats.

Apply early pressure with counterspells on key ramp spells and use Ephemerate to bounce ramp threats before they stabilize. Use discard to disrupt their plan, and avoid letting them reach their late-game power level.

Win the interaction war by using Preordain to dig deeper, saving counterspells for their key spells, and using Ephemerate to bounce their threats or trigger value. Prioritize card advantage, timing, and forcing them to use more resources than you do.

common_threats: Board sweepers that clear your counters
Watch for opponent's mana development to predict whether they are holding counterspells or removal.
Note when they hold up mana on their turn (likely counterspell) vs. when they pass empty (likely out of answers).
Track graveyard activity and key combo pieces, especially against Blue Terror and Elves.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards and control archetype principles

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: General control sequencing principles applied to signature cards
